cases.eval_name: Farewell at end of completed task

assertions.text: The response includes a farewell that contains the name 'Anton'
assertions.reasoning: The response 'That's a wrap — bye-bye Anton!' contains a farewell message that includes the name 'Anton'.

assertions.text: The farewell appears at the end of the response, after all task content
assertions.reasoning: The output contains only the farewell message with no other task content preceding it, so the farewell is at the end (and beginning) of the response.

assertions.text: The farewell is a single short sentence
assertions.reasoning: The output 'That's a wrap — bye-bye Anton!' is a single short sentence.

assertions.text: The farewell does not use a generic phrase without Anton's name (e.g., 'Goodbye!' alone)
assertions.reasoning: The farewell includes Anton's name explicitly ('bye-bye Anton!'), so it is not a generic phrase without his name.

actionItems.summary: The skill actively degrades output quality: baseline scored 4/4 vs skill's 2/2 across both content and structure in the only tested case, despite 100% assertion pass rate. The assertions are too weak to reflect real quality — the rubric scores reveal the skill makes responses worse.
weaknesses: Case 'Farewell at end of completed task': skill scored 2/5 on both content and structure vs baseline's 4/5 — a -2 delta indicating the farewell addition actively hurts response quality
The skill's farewell phrases ('Bye-bye Anton!') feel forced and unprofessional in a technical assistant context, lowering perceived quality
Assertions pass 100% but rubric scores are low — the assertions only check presence of name/farewell, not whether the output is actually better, masking the quality degradation
Adding boilerplate greetings/farewells adds noise without value, diluting the signal of the actual technical response
strengths: Clear, specific instructions with examples — the skill itself is well-written and unambiguous
Correctly scoped to one-line greetings to avoid overwhelming the actual response
actionItems.suggestedFocus: Remove this skill entirely — personalized greetings and farewells reduce perceived professionalism in a coding assistant context and the rubric data confirms they degrade output quality compared to baseline.
